business_impact.summary: Low technical risk intersects with Medium business criticality.

title: S3 general purpose buckets should have server access logging enabled
description: This control checks whether server access logging is enabled for an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket. The control fails if server access logging isn't enabled. When logging is enabled, Amazon S3 delivers access logs for a source bucket to a chosen target bucket. The target bucket must be in the same AWS Region as the source bucket and must not have a default retention period configured. The target logging bucket does not need to have server access logging enabled, and you should suppress findings for this bucket.
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether server access logging is enabled for an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket. The control fails if server access logging isn't enabled. When logging is enabled, Amazon S3 delivers access logs for a source bucket to a chosen target bucket. The target bucket must be in the same AWS Region as the source bucket and must not have a default retention period configured. The target logging bucket does not need to have server access logging enabled, and you should suppress findings for this bucket.
what_the_fix_does: Configures server access logging to improve traceability of bucket access.

findings.title: S3 general purpose buckets should have server access logging enabled

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_low__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'pr_only'. Risk is low and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.65, privilege_level=1.00, internet_exposure=0.00, matched_signals=keyword:log. Relationship context is incomplete, so the recommendation remains conservative and advisory unless policy overrides it.

execution_guidance.label: Enable S3 access logging (guided)
execution_guidance.blast_radius_summary: Targets AWS::::Account:696505809372 without intending account-wide mutation.

pre_checks.message: Confirm this change is intended for AWS::::Account:696505809372 in account 696505809372 (eu-north-1).
Confirm the repository, deployment path, and approver that own this infrastructure change before generating the PR bundle.
Provide the required strategy inputs before execution: log_bucket_name.
No specialized dependency checks are available for this strategy yet.

execution_guidance.expected_outcome: PR mode produces a reviewable infrastructure change: S3 server access logs will be enabled and delivered to a dedicated destination bucket. After merge and apply through your IaC path, Security Hub should reflect the remediated state within 12-24 hours.

post_checks.message: Review the generated bundle or diff, then capture plan and apply evidence after the change is merged through the normal infrastructure workflow.
Verify the completed change only affected the intended resource scope for AWS::::Account:696505809372.
Trigger immediate re-evaluation after the change lands and confirm the action closes without reopening.

rollback.summary: Use this rollback path to revert the infrastructure change through the same reviewed delivery workflow.
notes: Prefer reverting through version control and your normal deployment pipeline so rollback stays auditable.

attack_path_view.summary: Relationship context is incomplete, so the attack story stays fail-closed and bounded to directly observed evidence.
